sets.q: Which force always acts downward toward the center of the Earth on any object with mass?
sets.given:
This force acts on ALL objects with mass.
Formula: Fg = mg

sets.q: A coconut falls from a tree. Which force pulls it straight down?
sets.given: This is a non-contact force acting on all masses near Earth.

sets.q: What force causes a ball thrown upward to eventually come back down?
sets.given: Fg = mg, always directed toward Earth's center.

sets.q: Which force gives an object its weight?
sets.given: Weight = mass × gravitational acceleration (W = mg).

sets.q: A skydiver in free fall experiences which dominant force pulling them downward?
sets.given: Fg = mg, directed downward toward Earth's center.

sets.q: A book is sitting still on a table. The table pushes up on the book. What is this force called?
sets.given:
This force is a contact force.
It acts perpendicular to the surface.
It balances the weight of the object on flat ground.

sets.q: A box rests on the floor. The floor exerts an upward force on the box. What is this upward force?
sets.given: This contact force always acts perpendicular (normal) to the surface.

sets.q: A person stands still on a bridge. The bridge pushes upward on the person with a force equal to their weight. What type of force is this?
sets.given: This is a contact force perpendicular to the surface.

sets.q: What is the name of the contact force a surface exerts on an object, directed perpendicular to the surface?
sets.given: This force prevents objects from passing through surfaces.

sets.q: A laptop rests on a desk. Perpendicular to the desk surface, the desk exerts what force on the laptop?
sets.given: This force balances the weight when the surface is horizontal.

sets.q: You push a heavy crate across the floor. The floor resists your push and slows the crate. What force is acting against the motion?
sets.given:
This force opposes motion between two surfaces.
It acts in the OPPOSITE direction of movement.

sets.q: A sled slides down a snowy hill. What force from the snow opposes the sled's motion?
sets.given: This friction type acts on a moving object.

sets.q: A car skids to a stop on the road. What force between the tires and the road opposes the skidding motion?
sets.given: This friction acts when surfaces are sliding against each other.

sets.q: A book slides across a table and slows down. What force decelerates the book?
sets.given: This is friction between two surfaces in relative motion.

sets.q: Which type of friction opposes the motion of an object that is already sliding?
sets.given: There are two main types of friction: one for stationary objects, one for moving objects.

sets.q: A person tries to push a heavy refrigerator but it does not move. What force prevents the refrigerator from moving?
sets.given:
This type of friction acts when an object is at REST.
It prevents the object from beginning to move.

sets.q: You push against a wall and it does not move. What friction type resists your push?
sets.given: This friction prevents the initiation of motion.

sets.q: A file cabinet sits on a floor. You push gently and it doesn't budge. What force holds it in place?
sets.given: This force acts between surfaces at rest relative to each other.

sets.q: Before a box begins to slide, which friction force must be overcome?
sets.given: This type of friction is generally larger than kinetic friction.

sets.q: Which type of friction acts on a stationary car parked on a slope, preventing it from sliding?
sets.given: This friction prevents the onset of motion.

sets.q: A bucket is lifted by a rope. What is the force that the rope exerts on the bucket called?
sets.given:
This force is transmitted through a rope, cable, or string.
It acts along the rope, pulling the object toward the attachment point.

sets.q: A chandelier hangs from the ceiling by a wire. What force does the wire exert on the chandelier?
sets.given: This pulling force is transmitted through a flexible connector.

sets.q: A tightrope walker stands on a rope. The rope pulls the walker upward at an angle. What is this force?
sets.given: This force acts along a rope or cable.

sets.q: A car tows a trailer using a tow bar. What is the force the tow bar exerts on the trailer?
sets.given: This is the force transmitted through the connecting bar.

sets.q: A climber hangs from a rope. What type of force does the rope exert on the climber's harness?
sets.given: This force pulls the object toward the anchor point via the rope.

sets.q: Which of the following best defines 'applied force'?
sets.given: An applied force is one that a person or object directly exerts on another object.
opts: A force directly exerted on an object by a person or another object
The force of gravity on an object
The force a surface exerts perpendicular to an object
The resistance force between two surfaces

sets.q: A student pushes a shopping cart down an aisle. The student's push on the cart is an example of:
sets.given: This force involves direct contact and intentional action.

sets.q: Which of the following is the best example of an applied force?
sets.given: An applied force is directly exerted by a person or object.
opts: A hand pushing a door open

sets.q: When you kick a ball, the force your foot exerts on the ball is called:
sets.given: This force is directly exerted through contact.

sets.q: A construction worker hammers a nail. The force of the hammer on the nail is best described as:
sets.given: This is a deliberate contact force from one object to another.

sets.q: A spring is compressed and then pushes a toy car forward. What type of force does the spring exert?
sets.given:
This force is stored in elastic objects (springs, rubber bands).
Formula: F = kx (Hooke's Law)

sets.q: A rubber band is stretched and released, launching a paper ball. What force did the rubber band exert?
sets.given: This is the restoring force of an elastic object. F = kx.

sets.q: A trampoline surface is depressed and then springs back. What force causes the rebound?
sets.given: This force acts to restore an elastic material to its natural shape.

sets.q: A student uses a slingshot (elastic band) to launch a stone. The force from the slingshot is:
sets.given: F = kx — Hooke's Law describes this type of restoring force.

sets.q: A diving board bends when a diver stands on it and springs back when they jump. The restoring force of the board is:
sets.given: This force is proportional to the deformation of the material.

sets.q: An object is falling through the air and a force opposes its downward motion. What is this force?
sets.given:
This force acts on objects moving through a fluid (air or liquid).
It opposes the direction of motion.

sets.q: A sky diver reaches terminal velocity when the upward force on them balances gravity. What is that upward force?
sets.given: This force increases with speed and opposes motion through air.

sets.q: A feather falls more slowly than a rock because it experiences greater:
sets.given: This force from the air opposes the falling motion.

sets.q: A cyclist must pedal harder at high speeds because of increasing:
sets.given: This is the force exerted by the air on a moving object, opposing its motion.

sets.q: A badminton shuttlecock decelerates quickly in the air due to:
sets.given: This force opposes motion through any fluid medium.

sets.q: Two magnets attract each other without touching. What type of force is this an example of?
sets.given:
This force acts between objects without direct contact.
It can be attractive or repulsive.

sets.q: A compass needle aligns with Earth's field. The force that rotates the needle is:
sets.given: This is a non-contact force between magnetic materials.

sets.q: Two bar magnets repel each other when like poles are brought together. This is an example of:
sets.given: This non-contact force can be attractive or repulsive.

sets.q: A refrigerator magnet holds a paper note without any mechanical fastener. The force holding it is:
sets.given: This force acts through the air without contact between the note and magnet.

sets.q: Iron filings arrange in a pattern around a bar magnet. The force acting on the filings is:
sets.given: This is a non-contact force that creates a field around magnetic objects.

sets.q: Which pair correctly matches the force type with its definition?
sets.given:
Review all force definitions:
Normal = perpendicular contact force from a surface
Friction = opposes motion between surfaces
Tension = pull through a rope
Gravity = pull toward Earth's center
opts: Friction — opposes motion between two surfaces in contact
Normal Force — pulls objects toward Earth's center
Gravity — acts perpendicular to a surface

sets.q: Which statement correctly describes the normal force?
sets.given: Normal = perpendicular, Friction = opposing motion, Tension = along rope, Weight = downward.
opts: It acts perpendicular to the surface, pushing away from it
It pulls objects along a string

sets.q: Which of the following correctly describes tension?
sets.given: Tension is transmitted through ropes, strings, cables, or wires.
opts: A pulling force transmitted through a rope or string
A pushing force from a surface
A downward pull due to gravity

sets.q: A student says: 'Gravity is a contact force that acts perpendicular to a surface.' What is wrong with this statement?
sets.given: Review definitions: Gravity acts at a distance (non-contact), directed toward Earth's center.
opts: Gravity is non-contact and acts toward Earth's center, not perpendicular to surfaces
Nothing is wrong — gravity is a contact force
Gravity does act perpendicular to surfaces

sets.q: Which pair is INCORRECTLY matched?
sets.given: Normal force = perpendicular to surface; Friction = opposing motion; Tension = along rope; Gravity = toward Earth's center.
opts: Normal force — acts parallel to the surface
Tension — acts along a rope or string
